text.title: Tool Use — Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules: typed schemas, validate-before-execute, side-effect classification, human gate on irreversible, audit every call.

rule.statement: Every tool MUST have a JSON Schema for its arguments. Free-form string-only tool definitions are forbidden in production.

rationale.source: OpenAI/Anthropic tool docs; production agent post-mortems

rule.rationale: Typed schemas let constrained decoding return well-formed args. Free-form increases hallucination rate of arg shape to 10%+.

rule.statement: Dispatcher MUST validate model-emitted args against the tool's JSON Schema BEFORE calling the function. Bypassing validation is forbidden.
rule.rationale: LLMs hallucinate plausible-but-wrong args (wrong customer_id, out-of-range date, SQL injection). Validation is the only reliable guard.

rule.statement: Each tool MUST declare safety_class ∈ {read-only, mutating, destructive}. Dispatcher MUST refuse to execute tools whose class exceeds the agent's permission level.

rationale.source: permission boundary patterns; agent autonomy research

rule.rationale: Conflating read/write/delete in one permission grants the agent more power than needed. Explicit class enables least-privilege.

rule.statement: Tools with safety_class==destructive MUST sit behind a human review or scoped approval gate. Auto-execute of destructive tools is forbidden.

rationale.source: Anthropic responsible scaling; production incident reports

rule.rationale: Hallucinated destructive calls (DROP TABLE, mass delete, refund all) blow up faster than humans can roll back. Gate is the bright line.

rule.statement: Every tool dispatch MUST emit an audit log entry with timestamp, tool_name, args, caller (agent_id / user_id), and execution result. Silent dispatch is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Without audit, post-incident forensics is impossible. The agent's actions must be reconstructable from logs alone.
